adapter_guard_path_timing.pairing: Guarded and unchecked order is randomized per iteration across 15 deterministic valid adapter sessions; paired differences use per-call batch means. The unchecked path disables only camera-set and sensor-freshness guards; context-length validation remains active in both paths.
adapter_guard_path_timing.scope: in-process WOD2SimChallengeAdapter.drive_once_to_proto route-following path; includes state-to-input assembly, prediction, trajectory serialization, finite-output validation, reasoning parsing, and in-memory telemetry, but excludes gRPC transport, file I/O, simulator work, and human investigation

paired_comparison.scope: Descriptive paired counts for this designed suite; no independence-based significance test is applied.

design.control_construction: Fifteen unmodified, separately instantiated current-instrumentation adapter sessions, one paired with each fault operator.
design.fault_construction: One predefined telemetry or runtime-context mutation per source session; the detector receives only the mutated trace and context.
design.inference: No population-level confidence interval or hypothesis test is reported because sessions and mutations form a designed conformance suite.
design.scoring: Expected labels are retained by the experiment scorer and are not passed to diagnose_contract_trace.

source_trace.kind: dependency-light current-instrumentation adapter sessions; not external simulator rollouts

timing.pairing: Method order is randomized within every case and iteration; reported samples are per-call batch means.
timing.scope: in-memory detector execution on already-parsed telemetry and runtime context; excludes JSON parsing, file I/O, and human investigation time

external_compatibility.claim_boundary: External evaluator conformance evidence checks interface portability only. It is not a challenge submission, leaderboard result, policy-quality benchmark, or scenario-coverage claim.

failure_attribution.rule: Closed-loop behavior can be attributed to the policy only after the semantic route contract, temporal adapter, sensor-freshness audit, lifecycle state, deployment preconditions, and evidence gate pass. Public policy benchmark claims require the stricter benchmark gate. Rows outside the integration gate remain integration, precondition, evidence, or diagnostic records and cannot be counted as policy failures. A policy failure also requires the retained failure layer to be policy.

integration_effectiveness.rule: Integration-effectiveness is measured on executed closed-loop rows: completed full-contract rollouts are audited, and completed metric-bearing command-only rows are compared with a defined status-only acceptance baseline before route-invalid evidence is rejected.

does_not_support: reactive camera rendering or visual-policy evaluation
supports: live learned-policy/controller/physics feedback for a camera-blind model
service timing for this exact configuration
frozen-camera rejection for a camera-validating negative control

derived_surface.geometry: flat z=0 m rectangle x=[-100,200], y=[-100,100]

camera_map.content: raw AlpaSim map, metrics, and recorded seed camera; camera panel has no WOD2Sim text overlay

negative_control.diagnostic: RouteFollowingAlpaSimModel detected a frozen camera stream: ego pose changed from (0.1786, -0.0025, -0.002999) to (0.2308, -0.0024, -0.003765), and the latest camera timestamp advanced from 2056234417255 to 2056234517255, but the newest camera frame content did not change. The vehicle is moving while camera imagery is effectively frozen; check the upstream AlpaSim/sensorsim camera pipeline.

protocol_replay.claim_boundary: This is an executed client-to-service gRPC replay of recorded camera, route, egomotion, and Drive messages. It measures transport-inclusive driver RPC latency and contract diagnostics. Recorded inputs are non-reactive, so it does not measure simulator runtime, policy quality, human diagnosis time, or generalization to another integration framework. Route-following demonstrates the consequence of waypoint loss; the published NAVSIM EgoStatusMLP is a policy-signature negative control and does not consume route geometry.

learned_policy.family: NAVSIM v1.1 EgoStatusMLP blind learned baseline
learned_policy.name: ego_status_mlp_seed_0

release_scope.scope_rule: The public release core is the dependency-light contract adapter path using constant_velocity and route_following. Direct actor-aware planning, learned checkpoint execution, restricted scene redistribution, and a full policy benchmark are optional gated extensions, not release-core dependencies.

scenario_coverage.rule: Scenario-category coverage can be claimed only when authoritative metadata verifies each required category for the retained closed-loop scene set. Availability-selected unclassified scenes count as integration instances, not as coverage evidence.

H1_contract_sensitivity.criterion: route-following median endpoint divergence exceeds 0.1 m

H2_negative_control_invariance.criterion: all constant-velocity endpoint divergences are at most 1e-06 m

H3_attribution_correctness.criterion: only route-following with command-proxy geometry receives semantic.command_only

waymax_contract_study.claim_boundary: This is a deterministic policy-by-route negative-control experiment on the 20 scenarios bundled with the pinned Waymax checkout. It supports cross-runtime semantic-contract applicability and selective paired behavior consequences for this fixture. It is not a learned-policy benchmark, a representative WOMD sample, a safety comparison, a framework-superiority result, or a runtime-overhead measurement.

dataset.license: Waymax License Agreement for Non-Commercial Use
dataset.name: Waymax bundled WOMD route fixture

design.command_proxy_provenance: KEEP_HEADING is generated by the intervention operator; it is not read from the WOMD TFExample.

constant_velocity.controller: constant velocity in the current heading

route_conditions.command_proxy: original WOMD geometry replaced at the adapter boundary by a straight geometric proxy reconstructed from an intervention-defined KEEP_HEADING command

design.inference_scope: Paired descriptive evidence over the complete pinned fixture; the scenarios are not treated as a random sample from WOMD.
design.intervention: Within each policy, the paired route conditions share scenario, initial state, horizon, frequency, dynamics, non-SDC log playback, and controller implementation. The route-following policy receives either the native geometry or a degraded command-derived proxy. Constant velocity ignores the same mutated route field and is the negative control.

predeclared_checks.H1_contract_sensitivity: route-following median endpoint divergence exceeds 0.1 m
predeclared_checks.H2_negative_control_invariance: every constant-velocity endpoint divergence is at most 1e-06 m
predeclared_checks.H3_attribution_correctness: only route-following with command-proxy geometry is classified as a semantic-contract violation

design.primary_estimand: paired endpoint divergence(route_following) minus paired endpoint divergence(constant_velocity)
design.scientific_question: Can a contract-aware integration layer distinguish policy degradation caused by missing decision-relevant inputs from genuine policy failure?
